stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/LLM ecosystem, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama model family.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Evaluation Awareness Is Not One Capability: Evidence from Open Language Models
stories.author: Soundararajan Srinivasan
bullet_points: The study challenges the core assumption of safety benchmarks: that test-condition behavior predicts real-world deployment behavior.
It demonstrates that some Open Language Models exhibit 'evaluation awareness,' adapting their behavior when they detect testing cues.
This awareness creates a gap where benchmark compliance overstates a model's actual safety once the evaluation harness is removed.

stories.title: VibeThinker: 3B param model that beats Opus 4.5 on reasoning with novel SFT+GRPO
stories.author: timhigins
bullet_points: A new 3B parameter model named VibeThinker has been introduced.
The model demonstrates superior reasoning capabilities compared to Opus 4.5.
This performance improvement is attributed to a novel combination of SFT and GRPO techniques.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: GM installs robots at flagship EV factory after laying off 1,300 workers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: GM is implementing robotics at its flagship EV manufacturing facility.
The automation comes amidst significant workforce reductions, with 1,300 workers laid off.
Labor unions are expressing concern over the increasing automation trend, predicting a 'dark factory' future.

stories.title: NHTSA investigating alleged Tesla Autopilot crash that killed woman in her home
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: NHTSA is investigating a fatal crash involving a Tesla using Autopilot.
The incident occurred while the woman was in her home, raising questions about system usage.
The investigation comes as Tesla promotes Autopilot as a life-saving technology.

stories.title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: Introduces CoorDex, a new learning pipeline for continuous, dexterous humanoid locomotion and manipulation.
Addresses the limitation of current systems that treat loco-manipulation as a stop-and-go process.
Enables high-DoF dexterous manipulation while the humanoid is actively moving, moving beyond simple open-close grasps.

stories.title: AIR: Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code in MLLMs
stories.author: Yujie Zhong
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitations of current MLLMs, which often rely on predefined heuristics for visual tasks.
The paper introduces 'AIR' (Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code) to enhance MLLMs.
AIR aims to overcome the inability of existing methods to handle complex numerical computation problems by integrating code reasoning.

stories.title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: Introduces CoorDex, a new learning pipeline for continuous, dexterous humanoid locomotion and manipulation.
Addresses the limitation of current methods which treat loco-manipulation as a stop-and-go process.
Enables high-DoF dexterous manipulation while the humanoid is actively moving, moving beyond simple open-close grasps.

stories.title: Can LLMs Reliably Self-Report Adversarial Prefills, and How?
stories.author: Taegyoon Kim
bullet_points: The research investigates whether LLMs can reliably detect when their responses were manipulated by adversarial prefill attacks.
Testing across ten open-weight LLMs (3B to 70B) revealed that no model reliably recognizes its own compromised outputs.
Models demonstrated a low rate of claiming intent on prefilled responses, averaging only 27.3%.

stories.title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
stories.author: Kaiyan Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces EnterpriseClawBench, a new benchmark designed to test enterprise AI agents.
The benchmark is built from proprietary, real-world workplace sessions, reflecting actual business use cases.
It provides 852 reproducible tasks complete with fixtures, prompts, and semantic rubrics for rigorous evaluation.

stories.title: SVD-Surgeon: Optimal Singular-Value Surgery for Large Language Model Compression
stories.author: Frank Hutter
bullet_points: Introduces SVD-Surgeon, a new training-free method for compressing Large Language Models (LLMs).
Applies the Optimal Brain Surgeon (OBS) framework to the singular-value basis for targeted compression.
Addresses the critical deployment constraint of LLMs by optimizing low-rank compression via SVD.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Valve confirms it’s working with Intel and Nvidia on SteamOS for more GPUs
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Valve is collaborating with Intel and Nvidia regarding SteamOS.
The collaboration aims to improve GPU support within SteamOS.
This addresses the current limitation of handheld gaming PCs like MSI's Claw running Windows.

stories.title: Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without Ray-Ban
stories.author: Victoria Song
bullet_points: Meta has launched new smart glasses models that are distinct from the established Ray-Ban collaboration.
The new glasses are being positioned as a more accessible or cheaper alternative in the market.
The launch includes multiple styles and colors, signaling a broader product strategy from Meta.

stories.title: With Starfall, SpaceX eyes an edge in global cargo delivery from orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is developing 'Starfall' to facilitate the transport and delivery of goods from orbit.
The initiative focuses on establishing commercial capabilities for in-space logistics and cargo delivery.
This development signals a move toward utilizing space for broader commercial and logistical applications.

stories.title: Report: Kennedy Space Center not ready for era of super heavy rockets
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX plans an aggressive launch cadence of every eight days for Starship from Kennedy Space Center.
A report suggests the Kennedy Space Center infrastructure may not be prepared for this high-frequency operation.
The story highlights a potential bottleneck or readiness issue between ambitious private sector plans and existing government/facility capabilities.

stories.title: Polymarket's viral videos showed people winning big, but the bets were fake
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Polymarket's viral success was based on fraudulent activity.
The 'winning' bets were placed on cloned websites.
The bets, if placed on the real platform, would have resulted in losses.

stories.title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: AMD has reinstated memory encryption in its consumer CPUs following user complaints.
The decision was met with criticism, with some viewing it as a tactic to push consumers toward higher-cost chips.
This represents a notable response from AMD to community feedback regarding hardware security features.

stories.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
stories.author: Daniel Cohen-Or
bullet_points: The research addresses the issue of mode collapse in text-to-image models, where outputs lack diversity despite high visual fidelity.
The proposed method introduces 'controlled diversity' by enforcing structure on the generated samples.
This aims to move beyond incidental variations to allow for meaningful, controllable design choices in image generation.

stories.title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
stories.author: Lijun Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a theoretical gap in AdamW, the standard LLM optimizer, concerning its performance under heavy-tailed stochastic gradient noise.
Empirical evidence suggests LLM pretraining noise is heavy-tailed, challenging the finite-variance assumptions of current optimizer theory.
The research highlights that sign-based optimizers (Lion, Muon) and AdaGrad show promise in achieving convergence under these challenging noise conditions.

stories.title: Teaching LLMs String Matching, Backtracking, and Error Recovery to Deduce Bases and Truth Tables for the Combinatorially Exploding Bit Manipulation Puzzles
stories.author: Shubham Jain
bullet_points: The research addresses a known weakness of LLMs: difficulty with complex, combinatorial logic tasks like bit manipulation puzzles.
The paper introduces algorithmic innovations to help LLMs deduce hidden logical rules and truth tables from binary string inputs.
This work is presented in the context of the NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge, highlighting practical application in model evaluation.

stories.title: TailorMind: Towards Preference-Aligned Multimodal Content Generation
stories.author: Liqiang Nie
bullet_points: TailorMind addresses the challenge of personalized content generation when existing user-generated content (UGC) is unavailable or insufficient.
The research focuses on translating user behavioral traces directly into generation-ready preferences for multimodal content.
It proposes a method to create user-tailored multimodal content on demand, bypassing the need for pre-existing item pools.

stories.title: AI Exposure Scores: what they measure, what they miss, and what comes next
stories.author: Marzieh Fadaee
bullet_points: The article discusses 'AI Exposure Scores' developed by Eloundou et al. (2023) to quantify how much LLMs can assist with occupational tasks.
The scores are a significant empirical input into the ongoing 'future of work' debate.
The analysis highlights that the limitations of the original methodology are often overlooked as the scores are adopted elsewhere.

stories.title: AutoDex: An Automated Real-World System for Dexterous Grasping Data Collection
stories.author: Hanbyul Joo
bullet_points: AutoDex addresses the critical bottleneck in training dexterous grasping AI by automating real-world data collection.
It bridges the gap between slow, operator-biased teleoperation and unverified, scalable simulation data.
The system proposes a loop of generating candidate grasps and verifying them on physical hardware to ensure data validity.

stories.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
stories.author: Daniel Cohen-Or
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of mode collapse in text-to-image models, where outputs lack diversity.
Proposes a new method for 'controlled diversity' by enforcing structure on generated samples.
Aims to move beyond incidental variations to enable meaningful design choices in image generation.

stories.title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
stories.author: Lijun Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a theoretical gap in AdamW, the standard optimizer for LLMs, concerning its performance under heavy-tailed stochastic gradient noise.
Empirical evidence suggests LLM pretraining noise is heavy-tailed, challenging the finite-variance assumptions of current optimizer theory.
The research highlights that alternative optimizers like Lion, Muon, and AdaGrad show promise in achieving convergence under these heavy-tailed conditions.

stories.title: Tapered Language Models
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: Challenges the standard architectural assumption of uniformly distributed parameter capacity across all layers in modern language models.
Proposes that later layers in models like Transformers primarily refine the residual stream rather than performing wholesale transformations.
Investigates whether parameter allocation within the model depth should be non-uniform based on functional evidence.

stories.title: On the Limits of Prompt-Conditioned Language Models as General-Purpose Learners
stories.author: Jun Wang
bullet_points: Challenges the prevailing view of LLMs as truly general-purpose learners.
Proposes that language acts as a capacity-limited interface for task information.
Analyzes prompt-conditioned interaction using a bilevel 'cheap-talk' game framework.

stories.title: MAS-PromptBench: When Does Prompt Optimization Improve Multi-Agent LLM Systems?
stories.author: Laixi Shi
bullet_points: The research focuses on Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) as a scalable approach for agentic AI, involving multiple LLM agents.
System prompts are identified as a critical and accessible optimization surface for improving MAS behavior without requiring model finetuning.
The paper investigates when prompt optimization actually leads to performance improvements in complex, multi-agent LLM workflows.

stories.title: Action-BED: Task-Driven Bayesian Experimental Design with Singly Intractable Objectives
stories.author: Tom Rainforth
bullet_points: Introduces Action-BED, a new approach to Bayesian Experimental Design (BED).
Addresses the major limitation of traditional BED, which involves doubly intractable optimization objectives.
Reformulates BED based on Expected Future Loss (EFL) derived from first principles decision theory.

stories.title: Randomized YaRN Improves Length Generalization for Long-Context Reasoning
stories.author: Greg Durrett
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of LLMs struggling to generalize to very long sequences after initial training.
It introduces 'Randomized YaRN,' a new training method combining YaRN positional extrapolation with randomized positional encoding and a length curriculum.
The goal of the method is to significantly improve the length generalization capabilities of large language models for long-context reasoning.

stories.title: LangMAP: A Language-Adaptive Approach to Tokenization
stories.author: Tiago Pimentel
bullet_points: Introduces LangMAP, a new language-adaptive tokenization scheme.
Addresses the high cost of using language-specific tokenizers (training from scratch or vocabulary adaptation).
Extends the UnigramLM algorithm to effectively generate language-specific tokenization from a single model.

stories.title: The Energy Consumption of Transformer Fine-Tuning: A Roofline-Inspired Scaling Model
stories.author: Mansour Zoubeirou a Mayaki
bullet_points: Addresses the growing computational and energy costs associated with large Transformer models in NLP.
Introduces a framework to model the energy consumption of Transformer training across multiple GPUs.
Uses controlled architectural sweeps of BERT models to relate measured energy to lightweight proxies.

stories.title: VeriEvol: Scaling Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning via Verifiable Evol-Instruct
stories.author: Yujiu Yang
bullet_points: Introduces 'VeriEvol,' a method to scale multimodal mathematical reasoning in AI.
Addresses the critical issue of maintaining reward label reliability as training data volume increases.
Decouples prompt difficulty and data construction verification before any policy updates in RL.

stories.title: Self-Compacting Language Model Agents
stories.author: Daniel Khashabi
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of context window overflow in long-running AI agent traces (chains of thought/tool calls).
Critiques existing compaction methods for being rigid and structure-agnostic, risking data loss.
Introduces 'SelfCompact,' a novel scaffold enabling the language model to autonomously decide when and how to perform content compaction.

stories.title: AI found 300 WordPress plugin zero-days in 72 hours. I build plugins. Here's what changed for me.
stories.author: rapls
bullet_points: AI tools were used to discover a significant number of zero-day vulnerabilities (300) in WordPress plugins within a short timeframe.
The article discusses the personal impact and necessary changes for a plugin developer in response to these security findings.
It highlights the practical application of AI in security auditing, specifically in the context of WordPress plugin development.

stories.title: The 80/20 Rule of AI Code — Why the Last 20% Takes 80% of Your Time
stories.author: harsh2644
bullet_points: The article discusses the practical reality of using AI code generation.
It highlights the disproportionate time spent on the final 20% of code development.
The core theme is the shift in developer workflow due to AI assistance.

stories.title: Agents write code, but they don't remember
stories.author: lizziepika
bullet_points: The article argues that while AI agents are good at code generation, they currently lack persistent memory.
It posits a shift in the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) where intent drives development, and code becomes a secondary layer.
The core problem highlighted is the loss of reasoning context when an agent's thought process is not retained.

stories.title: Hetzner Doubled Its Prices Again. The AI Memory Crunch Is Why
stories.author: devopsdaily
bullet_points: Hetzner, a hosting provider, has increased its service prices again.
The primary driver cited for the price hikes is the growing demand and resource strain from AI applications (the 'AI Memory Crunch').
The news highlights the increasing operational costs associated with scaling AI infrastructure.

stories.title: If AI Replaces Juniors, Who Replaces Seniors? The Hidden Cost of Misusing AI in Tech
stories.author: oninross
bullet_points: The article addresses the growing concern over AI's impact on the tech workforce, specifically questioning the future role of senior developers.
It critiques the current industry trend of overhyping AI tools, referencing the rise of 'vibe coders' on platforms like LinkedIn and Medium.
The core theme is the 'hidden cost' associated with the improper or uncritical application of AI in software development.

stories.title: Stop Paying for GitHub Copilot: Build a Free, 100% Private AI Assistant Locally
stories.author: johnnylemonny
bullet_points: The article proposes an alternative to paid AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot.
The core value proposition is building a free, locally-run, and 100% private AI assistant.
It addresses the growing industry trend of shifting away from cloud-based AI subscriptions.
